summary: End-to-end testing of TagIT MVP. 33/33 pytest backend tests pass. All critical frontend flows verified via Playwright on the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL: landing, JWT register+login, dashboard (3 seeded tags + stats 3/3/3), tag edit/save, QR + 3 PDF layouts (a4/id/keyring), public finder, finder message → inbox, medical emergency view (tel: link + blood group/allergies/contact/police station), i18n switch to Hindi (Devanagari), settings export download, logout. Multi-tenant isolation verified.

issues: Stat tiles show em-dash placeholder for ~1-2s while /api/tags + /api/inbox load. Acceptable, but a skeleton/spinner instead of '—' would feel more polished.

critical_code_review_comments: auth_routes.google_session: synchronous `requests.get` inside async route blocks the event loop. Switch to httpx.AsyncClient for non-blocking I/O.
message_routes.post_finder_message: rate-limit query uses ISO-date prefix `created_at[:10]` for $gte which compares strings — works but is fragile. Prefer a proper datetime range.
tag_routes.get_public_tag inserts a scan on EVERY GET — a curious finder hitting refresh will pollute counts. Consider de-duping by (tag_id, ip_hash) within ~30s window.
server.py uses deprecated `@app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown')`. Migrate to FastAPI lifespan context manager.
auth_routes.delete_me has no double-confirmation step server-side (relies entirely on UI). For a destructive RTBF op consider requiring current_password in body.
test_credentials: Admin: anand@tagit.in / TagITAdmin@2026 (seeded). Second user auto-created in tests with random email.
seed_data_creation: Backend boot seed auto-creates admin + 3 demo tags (vehicle, pet, medical with emergency profile). No manual seeding needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Slugs for seeded tags are randomly generated per fresh DB; fetch them via GET /api/tags after admin login rather than hardcoding. The honeypot field on the finder only appears inside the extended 'send message' form (not on initial quick-action buttons) — backend correctly drops bot_check submissions (verified via API). Emergent Google /api/auth/google/session endpoint correctly returns 401 for invalid session_id (cannot do full OAuth round-trip without real browser flow).
rca of the issue: No bugs found — all features behave as specified in the review request.
